title: GCC countries hit 100% vaccine coverage as Gulf states invest $842.7m in health research - Arabian Business:
summary: GCC countries achieved 100 per cent essential vaccine coverage in 2024, compared with a global average of around 84 per cent
thbAction: Map the news to THB's provider CRM for referral growth, patient lifecycle engagement, and multi-facility relationship management across hospital and provider networks.
contentHtml:
Skip to content

GCC countries achieved full coverage for essential vaccines during 2024, significantly outperforming the global average and highlighting the strength of public health systems across the region, according to new data released by the Statistical Centre for the Cooperation Council for the Arab States of the Gulf.

The data showed essential vaccine coverage across GCC countries reached 100 per cent during 2024, compared with approximately 84 per cent globally.

The figures were published in the Statistical Centre’s weekly bulletin issued to mark World Health Day 2026.

According to the bulletin, the results reflect the effectiveness of national immunisation programmes and ongoing investment in healthcare infrastructure across GCC countries.

The Statistical Centre said Gulf countries continue strengthening their role in global healthcare through:

  • Investment in medical research and development
  • Expanding access to affordable medicines and vaccines
  • Enhancing international health cooperation

GCC health systems  

The report said sustained investment in healthcare infrastructure and international partnerships remains central to Gulf health strategies aimed at achieving universal health coverage and supporting long-term public health sustainability.

The bulletin also highlighted the GCC’s contribution to international healthcare financing and medical research through Official Development Assistance (ODA).

According to the data, GCC countries contributed approximately $842.7m to financing the health sector and medical research during 2023.

The figure represented approximately 4.6 per cent of total international development assistance.

The report said the contribution demonstrates the GCC countries’ commitment to supporting global health systems, expanding medical knowledge transfer, supporting access to medicines and vaccines and enhancing healthcare resilience in developing countries.

The Statistical Centre said the initiatives align with the Sustainable Development Goals, particularly Goal 3 focused on ensuring healthy lives and promoting well-being for all age groups.

The bulletin highlighted scientific research as a key pillar for improving healthcare quality and strengthening public health systems.

According to the report, GCC countries also continue supporting developing nations through efforts aimed at strengthening healthcare systems and improving their ability to respond to health challenges.

The bulletin said Gulf countries remain active partners in initiatives designed to expand vaccine and medicine access across different population groups worldwide.
